explicit_text: I was born in Benham Hospital in 1946 and attended grade school there. Benham was a close-knit community where events like the Fourth of July, sponsored by the company, were big deals. My older brother Johnny played football for Benham High School, and two of my sisters were cheerleaders there. Losing the Benham school after my freshman year was tough because I felt like you missed out if you didn't go to school there.
implicit_text: I was born in the local hospital in 1946 and attended grade school in that small mining town. It was a close-knit community where events like the Fourth of July, sponsored by the mining company, were big deals. My older brother Johnny played football for the local high school, and two of my sisters were cheerleaders there. Losing the town's school after my freshman year was tough because I felt like you missed out if you didn't go to school there.

explicit_text: I spent my sophomore and senior years of high school in Cumberland, Kentucky, after Benham's school closed. It was in Cumberland that I met my wife, Betty, and we became high school sweethearts. I graduated from Cumberland High School in 1964, and Cumberland played a big role in my teenage years and early adult life.
implicit_text: I spent my sophomore and senior years of high school in the neighboring coal town after the school in my hometown closed. It was in that town where I met my wife, Betty, and we became high school sweethearts. I graduated from the high school there in 1964, and that place played a big role in my teenage years and early adult life.

explicit_text: I attended Lynch High School for my junior year because I had an opportunity to play basketball there. Unfortunately, injuries prevented me from playing as I hoped, so I ended up returning to Cumberland to finish high school. Lynch was part of a big rivalry with Cumberland and Benham, so moving between these towns for school was a significant experience.
implicit_text: I attended the high school in the nearby mining town for my junior year because I had an opportunity to play basketball there. Unfortunately, injuries prevented me from playing as I hoped, so I ended up returning to the previous town to finish high school. That town was part of a big rivalry with my other two hometowns, so moving between these places for school was a significant experience.

explicit_text: My father worked for International Harvester as a shuttle car operator in the mines near Benham, starting before the Depression. I joined International Harvester myself on September 14, 1966, as a machine operator and later became a union representative for the United Auto Workers. The company sponsored major community events like the Fourth of July celebrations, which were important to the town.
implicit_text: My father worked for a major agricultural and construction equipment manufacturer as a shuttle car operator in the mines near my hometown, starting before the Depression. I joined the same company myself on September 14, 1966, as a machine operator and later became a union representative for the United Auto Workers. The company sponsored major community events like the Fourth of July celebrations, which were important to the town.

explicit_text: The Credit Union was a big part of our community social life, especially the annual meetings which were major social events. Although family members couldn't be members early on, they were allowed to attend the meetings where they handed out silver dollars and finger foods. I remember one year receiving a 1900 silver dollar, which I swapped with my dad for one from his birth year, 1912.
implicit_text: The local financial cooperative was a big part of our community social life, especially the annual meetings which were major social events. Although family members couldn't be members early on, they were allowed to attend the meetings where they handed out silver dollars and finger foods. I remember one year receiving a 1900 silver dollar, which I swapped with my dad for one from his birth year, 1912.

explicit_text: The Fourth of July was a huge event in Benham, sponsored by International Harvester. They provided entertainment, a big ice cream truck, and held the celebration at the football field. The whole town was pretty much invited, and it was one of the most memorable events from my school days.
implicit_text: The Fourth of July was a huge event in my hometown, sponsored by the major equipment company. They provided entertainment, a big ice cream truck, and held the celebration at the football field. The whole town was pretty much invited, and it was one of the most memorable events from my school days.

explicit_text: In what I believe was 1965, I worked for the Scotia coal company for about six months, assisting engineers with coal samples from abandoned mines. It was a seasonal and unpleasant job, and after it ended, I chose to move on to work at International Harvester. Scotia was a subsidiary of Blue Diamond Coal Company and part of my early work experience.
implicit_text: In what I believe was 1965, I worked for a regional coal company for about six months, assisting engineers with coal samples from abandoned mines. It was a seasonal and unpleasant job, and after it ended, I chose to move on to work at the major equipment manufacturer. That coal company was a subsidiary of a larger coal operator and part of my early work experience.
cues: subsidiary of Blue Diamond Coal Company

explicit_text: The Blue Diamond Coal Company was the parent company of Scotia coal company where I briefly worked. While I didn't work directly for Blue Diamond, my experience at Scotia connected me to their operations. This was part of the coal mining industry in Kentucky during the 1960s.
implicit_text: The larger coal operator was the parent company of the regional coal company where I briefly worked. While I didn't work directly for the parent company, my experience at the subsidiary connected me to their operations. This was part of the coal mining industry in Kentucky during the 1960s.

explicit_text: After high school, I worked for about eighteen months at McCall Corporation in Dayton, Ohio, in their bindery and pattern shop. McCall's published women's magazines including Redbook, U.S. News & World Report, and Reader's Digest. It was a tough job working alone in the basement doing stitching, and although it was a valuable experience, it was the longest eighteen months of my life.
implicit_text: After high school, I worked for about eighteen months at a well-known publishing company in Dayton, Ohio, in their bindery and pattern shop. This company published women's magazines including Redbook, U.S. News & World Report, and Reader's Digest. It was a tough job working alone in the basement doing stitching, and although it was a valuable experience, it was the longest eighteen months of my life.

explicit_text: Redbook was one of the magazines published by McCall Corporation where I worked after high school. Though I didn't work directly on the magazine content, I was involved in the bindery and pattern shop that supported publications like Redbook. It was part of the printing industry experience I had before moving on to other jobs.
implicit_text: One of the magazines published by the company where I worked after high school was a popular women's magazine. Though I didn't work directly on the magazine content, I was involved in the bindery and pattern shop that supported publications like that. It was part of the printing industry experience I had before moving on to other jobs.

explicit_text: U.S. News & World Report was another publication produced by McCall Corporation during my time there. Like with Redbook and Reader's Digest, my work was behind the scenes in the bindery and pattern shop supporting these magazines. It was part of the broader printing industry experience I gained in Dayton, Ohio.
implicit_text: Another publication produced by the company during my time there was a well-known news magazine. Like with the other magazines, my work was behind the scenes in the bindery and pattern shop supporting these publications. It was part of the broader printing industry experience I gained in Dayton, Ohio.

explicit_text: Reader's Digest was among the well-known magazines published by McCall Corporation when I worked there. My role was in the bindery and pattern shop, so while I didn't handle the magazine content, I was part of the production process. This job was part of my early career before returning to work in Kentucky.
implicit_text: Among the well-known magazines published by the company when I worked there was a widely read digest magazine. My role was in the bindery and pattern shop, so while I didn't handle the magazine content, I was part of the production process. This job was part of my early career before returning to work in Kentucky.

explicit_text: I was a member of the United Auto Workers union at International Harvester starting from about my third year there. I served as a union representative for most of my career, which lasted twenty-seven years. The UAW was distinct from the United Mine Workers and was the union representing us at the factory rather than the mines.
implicit_text: I was a member of the United Auto Workers union at the manufacturing plant starting from about my third year there. I served as a union representative for most of my career, which lasted twenty-seven years. This union was distinct from the miners' union and was the union representing us at the factory rather than the mines.

explicit_text: The United Mine Workers was the largest union representing coal miners, including my father who worked in the mines. It was different from the United Auto Workers, which I joined later at International Harvester. There was some animosity between the United Mine Workers and the Progressive company union, which was seen as a company-controlled group.
implicit_text: The largest union representing coal miners, including my father who worked in the mines, was different from the union I joined later at the equipment manufacturing plant. There was some animosity between that miners' union and the company-controlled union group, which was seen as a company-controlled group.

explicit_text: My favorite song in high school was 'A Summer Place,' which was both instrumental and vocal. It was also the theme of our senior prom, which we celebrated in 1964. We even had a fiftieth anniversary reunion a few years ago where that song was still remembered fondly.
